# naics_classifications.feather

## Description
Complete mapping of NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) codes to their descriptive titles. Includes the full hierarchy from Sector (2-digit) to National Industry (6-digit) levels.

This dataset provides descriptive titles for all NAICS classification codes used in the Compustat database. It can be used to map NAICS codes in the main dataset to their human-readable names.

## Generated by
`src/pre_query/compustat/download_industry_classifications.py`

## Variables

### naics
NAICS classification code. String type, zero-padded to 6 digits. The code length indicates the hierarchy level:
- 2 digits: Sector (e.g., "23" = Construction)
- 3 digits: Subsector (e.g., "236" = Construction of Buildings)
- 4 digits: Industry Group (e.g., "2361" = Residential Building Construction)
- 5 digits: Industry (e.g., "23611" = Residential Building Construction)
- 6 digits: National Industry (e.g., "236117" = New Housing For-Sale Builders)

### naicsdesc
NAICS classification description/title. String type. The human-readable name for the classification code (e.g., "Construction", "Residential Building Construction").

### level
Classification level (number of significant digits). Integer type. Calculated from the length of the code after removing leading zeros:
- 2: Sector
- 3: Subsector
- 4: Industry Group
- 5: Industry
- 6: National Industry
